dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8843 IH: Federal Accountability for Felony Obstruction Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. McGuire (for himself, Mr. Hunt, and Mr. Van Epps) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States, to include property damage in acts that constitute domestic terrorism, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Accountability for Felony Obstruction Act or the FAFO Act.

section.header: Inclusion of property damage in definition of domestic terrorism
section.text: Section 2331(5)(A) of title 18, United States Code, is amended to read as follows:

clause.text: are a violation of the criminal laws of the United States or of any State, and that are dangerous to human life or are likely to cause significant damage to property; and

subclause.text: a law enforcement officer or a first responder in carrying out their duties; or
